heading: Minimum State law requirements
label: 8005 Minimum State law requirements

children.label: i
children.text: the enclosure of all outdoor residential pools and spas by barriers to entry that will effectively prevent small children from gaining unsupervised and unfettered access to the pool or spa; and

children.label: I
children.text: more than 1 drain;

children.label: II
children.text: 1 or more unblockable drains; or

children.label: III
children.text: no main drain; and

children.label: B
children.text: the State meets such additional State law requirements for pools and spas as the Commission may establish after public notice and a 30-day public comment period.

children.heading: Use of minimum State law requirements
children.label: 2 Use of minimum State law requirements
A
children.text: shall use the minimum State law requirements under paragraph (1) solely for the purpose of determining the eligibility of a covered entity for a grant under ; andsection 8004 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: may not enforce any requirement under paragraph (1) except for the purpose of determining the eligibility of a covered entity for a grant under .section 8004 of this title

children.heading: Requirements to reflect national performance standards and Commission guidelines
children.label: 3 Requirements to reflect national performance standards and Commission guidelines
A
children.text: consider current or revised national performance standards on pool and spa barrier protection and entrapment prevention; and

children.label: B
children.text: ensure that any such requirements are consistent with the guidelines contained in the Commission’s publication 362, entitled “Safety Barrier Guidelines for Home Pools”, the Commission’s publication entitled “Guidelines for Entrapment Hazards: Making Pools and Spas Safer”, and any other pool safety guidelines established by the Commission.

children.heading: Standards
children.label: b Standards
children.text: Nothing in this section prevents the Commission from promulgating standards regulating pool and spa safety or from relying on an applicable national performance standard.

children.heading: Basic access-related safety devices and equipment requirements to be considered
children.label: c Basic access-related safety devices and equipment requirements to be considered

children.heading: Covers
children.label: 1 Covers
children.text: A safety pool cover.

children.heading: Gates
children.label: 2 Gates
children.text: A gate with direct access to the swimming pool or spa that is equipped with a self-closing, self-latching device.

children.heading: Doors
children.label: 3 Doors
children.text: Any door with direct access to the swimming pool or spa that is equipped with an audible alert device or alarm which sounds when the door is opened.

children.heading: Pool alarm
children.label: 4 Pool alarm
children.text: A device designed to provide rapid detection of an entry into the water of a swimming pool or spa.

children.heading: Entrapment, entanglement, and evisceration prevention standards to be required
children.label: d Entrapment, entanglement, and evisceration prevention standards to be required

children.heading: Safety vacuum release system
children.label: A Safety vacuum release system
children.text: A safety vacuum release system which ceases operation of the pump, reverses the circulation flow, or otherwise provides a vacuum release at a suction outlet when a blockage is detected, that has been tested by an independent third party and found to conform to ASME/ANSI standard A112.19.17 or ASTM standard F2387, or any successor standard.

children.heading: Suction-limiting vent system
children.label: B Suction-limiting vent system
children.text: A suction-limiting vent system with a tamper-resistant atmospheric opening.

children.heading: Gravity drainage system
children.label: C Gravity drainage system
children.text: A gravity drainage system that utilizes a collector tank.

children.heading: Automatic pump shut-off system
children.label: D Automatic pump shut-off system
children.text: An automatic pump shut-off system.

children.heading: Drain disablement
children.label: E Drain disablement
children.text: A device or system that disables the drain.

children.heading: Other systems
children.label: F Other systems
children.text: Any other system determined by the Commission to be equally effective as, or better than, the systems described in subparagraphs (A) through (E) of this paragraph at preventing or eliminating the risk of injury or death associated with pool drainage systems.

children.heading: Applicable standards
children.label: 2 Applicable standards
children.text: Any device or system described in subparagraphs (B) through (E) of paragraph (1) shall meet the requirements of any ASME/ANSI or ASTM performance standard if there is such a standard for such a device or system, or any applicable consumer product safety standard.

children.heading: State defined
children.label: e State defined
children.text: In this section, the term “State” includes an Indian Tribe.
